summary: Renamed multiple HSD_ObjAllocData variables from address-style or vague names to purpose-oriented names across fighter and item code, plus matching asm references, headers, docs, and GALE01 symbols. The PR also renamed Fighter.x2D8_specialAttributes2 to dat_attrs_backup, introduced a typed Fighter_x59C_t wrapper for the 0x8000 allocation at fp+0x59C/fp+0x5A0, and renamed Crazy Hand dat attributes to ftCrazyHand_DatAttrs. No PR body or review comments were present, so rationale is inferred from the diff: clearer allocator and data-attribute naming without behavioral changes.
what_changed: Renamed fighter allocator globals: Fighter_80458FFC to fighter_dat_attrs_alloc_data, fighter_bones_alloc_data to fighter_parts_alloc_data, Fighter_80459054 to fighter_dobj_list_alloc_data, Fighter_80459080 to fighter_x2040_alloc_data, and Fighter_804590AC to fighter_x59C_alloc_data.
Updated HSD_ObjAllocInit, HSD_ObjAlloc, and HSD_ObjFree uses in fighter.c, ftdata.c, ftdemo.c, Kirby/Purin/Crazy Hand character code, and related assembly to use the new allocator symbols.
Renamed item allocator symbols: itemAllocData to item_alloc_data, unkAllocData1 to item_dynamic_bones_alloc_data, and Item_804A0C38 to item_link_alloc_data.

Updated item allocation/free sites and assembly references for link-like item allocations across itlinkhookshot, itclimbersstring, itnessyoyo, itsamusgrapple, and itseakchain assembly, plus item headers/docs.

Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to keep the renamed symbols bound to their original BSS addresses, including fighter allocator addresses 0x80458FFC through 0x804590AC and item allocator addresses 0x804A0BE0 through 0x804A0C38.
Renamed Fighter field x2D8_specialAttributes2 to dat_attrs_backup and updated PUSH_ATTRS to use that name.
Added struct Fighter_x59C_t with size 0x8000 and changed Fighter.x59C/x5A0 from UNK_T to struct Fighter_x59C_t*.
Renamed Crazy Hand attribute typedef ftCrazyHandAttributes to ftCrazyHand_DatAttrs and updated PUSH_ATTRS/COPY_ATTRS usage.
smart_moves: Kept symbols.txt address mappings intact while renaming symbols, which preserves linker identity and makes the rename searchable by both old address and new purpose.
Updated assembly @ha/@l references in parallel with C declarations, avoiding stale labels in non-decompiled files.

Used allocator names that encode either the allocated object role, such as fighter_parts_alloc_data and item_dynamic_bones_alloc_data, or the owning field offset when the exact semantic purpose is still unclear, such as fighter_x2040_alloc_data and fighter_x59C_alloc_data.
Renamed the fp+0x2D8 backup field together with the allocator that services it, making the PUSH_ATTRS macro less misleading.
Added a size-checked placeholder struct for the large x59C allocation instead of leaving fp->x59C and fp->x5A0 as untyped UNK_T.
decomp_lessons: When an HSD_ObjAllocData instance is identified by allocation size and call sites, rename the allocator itself and every alloc/free user in the same PR; stale allocator names make data-lifetime analysis harder.
If a global object's true semantic name is unknown but its owner field is known, a field-offset name like fighter_x2040_alloc_data is preferable to a raw address name like Fighter_80459080.
Allocator globals often need synchronized edits in C source, public headers, dox files, symbols.txt, and hand-written assembly; search for @ha/@l forms as well as plain C identifiers.
Typing an allocated buffer with a size-asserted placeholder struct can document allocator size and improve field types without claiming unknown internal layout.
Renaming a backing allocation can expose adjacent field names: fighter_dat_attrs_alloc_data supported renaming x2D8_specialAttributes2 to dat_attrs_backup.
For local static allocators, preserve local scope in symbols.txt while still replacing camelCase or unknown names with project-style snake_case.
naming_conventions: HSD_ObjAllocData globals use snake_case with an _alloc_data suffix.
Fighter allocator names begin with fighter_, e.g. fighter_dat_attrs_alloc_data, fighter_parts_alloc_data, fighter_dobj_list_alloc_data, fighter_x2040_alloc_data, fighter_x59C_alloc_data.
Item allocator names begin with item_, e.g. item_alloc_data, item_dynamic_bones_alloc_data, item_link_alloc_data.
Unknown-but-field-associated fighter allocators can use the field offset in the name, as in fighter_x2040_alloc_data and fighter_x59C_alloc_data.
DAT attribute typedefs can use a _DatAttrs suffix, as shown by ftCrazyHand_DatAttrs.
Avoid address-derived names such as Fighter_80458FFC, Fighter_80459054, Fighter_80459080, Fighter_804590AC, and Item_804A0C38 when a role-based name is available.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Updated PowerPC assembly symbol operands without changing instruction bytes or addresses, e.g. Fighter_80459080@ha/@l became fighter_x2040_alloc_data@ha/@l in ftKb_Init.s.
Updated item assembly references to Item_804A0C38@ha/@l so link-style item allocations now reference item_link_alloc_data@ha/@l.
The diff is mostly balanced additions/deletions, indicating a symbol-only/name-only change intended to preserve matching.
Kept BSS addresses unchanged in config/GALE01/symbols.txt while changing labels, which is the safe way to rename globals in a matching decomp project.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
Because there was no PR body, the exact reviewer/author rationale is not directly stated; conclusions are based on changed identifiers and allocation sizes/call sites.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename fighter_x2040_alloc_data or determine the real semantic purpose of fp->x2040.
Find later PRs that define the internal layout of Fighter_x59C_t or rename fighter_x59C_alloc_data.
Search for remaining HSD_ObjAllocData globals with address-derived names like Fighter_804xxxxx or Item_804xxxxx.
Search for remaining fields named x2D8_specialAttributes2 or uses of dat_attrs_backup to confirm migration completeness.
Look for additional character dat attribute typedefs that should follow the _DatAttrs naming convention.
